county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from York County in 1800. Site of the pivotal 1863 Battle of Gettysburg. Scotch-Irish and German settlement predominated. Agricultural county with strong Civil War heritage. Gettysburg National Military Park draws genealogists researching soldiers from across the Union and Confederacy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Adams County research centers on the Adams County Historical Society in Gettysburg, which maintains extensive Civil War-era records, family files, and cemetery transcriptions. The Register and Recorder office holds deeds from 1800 and wills from the same year. For pre-1800 families, check York County records at the York County Archives. German Reformed and Lutheran church records are critical for 18th-century families, available on FamilySearch microfilm. The Scotch-Irish heritage is strong in western townships; check Presbyterian church records. Gettysburg College Special Collections has local manuscript materials including the Samuel Schmucker papers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed indexes and images from 1800; online search available

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation. Grantor-grantee indexes.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories, Orphans Court records from 1800.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete. FamilySearch microfilm available for early volumes.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Adams County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885. Free digital access through PA State Archives.
Death certificates 1906-1968, birth certificates 1906-1913 free online.

source_profiles.source_name: German Reformed and Lutheran Records — Adams Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German-heritage congregations. Christ Lutheran, St. James Reformed in Gettysburg.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival. Many microfilmed by FamilySearch.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1800. Agricultural schedules 1850-1880 document farming community.
Muster rolls, compiled service records, pension files. Gettysburg battlefield records extensive.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent. PA Archives has digitized Civil War muster rolls.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1788 from Westmoreland and Washington counties. Contains Pittsburgh, the 'Steel City' at the confluence of the Allegheny and Monongahela rivers. Major industrial center from 1850s. Massive immigration waves: Irish, German, Eastern European, Italian, African American (Great Migration). Second-most populous PA county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Allegheny County research requires navigating both county and city of Pittsburgh records. The Western Pennsylvania Genealogical Society (WPGS) in Oakland is indispensable with its extensive surname files, church record abstracts, and cemetery indexes. The Heinz History Center holds industrial records including Carnegie Steel Company files and immigration documentation. County deed records from 1788 are at the Recorder of Deeds with full online access. Pittsburgh City Archives has municipal records. For African American research, check the August Wilson African American Cultural Center and WPGS African American committee files. The massive ethnic diversity means church records span Catholic parishes (Polish, Italian, Croatian, Slovak, Irish), Orthodox churches, synagogues, and Black Baptist and AME congregations.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Full deed search system online from 1788; grantor-grantee indexes and document images

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Comprehensive deed and mortgage records with full online search. One of PA's best online systems.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories. Orphans Court records from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete. Early volumes in good condition.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Allegheny County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications. Free digital access.
Death certificates 1906-1968, birth certificates 1906-1913.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete for covered period. Pittsburgh had earlier registration from 1870.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1790. Industrial and manufacturing schedules document steel and iron works.
Multi-ethnic church records: Catholic (Polish, Italian, Slovak, Irish parishes), Lutheran, Presbyterian, Baptist, AME. WPGS has extensive abstracts.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Variable by parish. WPGS has compiled indexes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pittsburgh Post-Gazette (from 1786), Press, Sun-Telegraph. Obituaries, vital notices, immigration stories.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent coverage. Post-Gazette one of oldest US papers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny, Lycoming, and Westmoreland counties. Named for Colonel John Armstrong who led the 1756 expedition against Kittanning. Rural county along the Allegheny River. Iron furnaces and glass manufacturing in 19th century. Coal mining significant in southern townships.
county_metadata.research_tips: Armstrong County Recorder of Deeds in Kittanning has records from 1800. The Armstrong County Historical Museum and Genealogical Society maintains family files, cemetery transcriptions, and church record abstracts. For pre-1800 research, check Allegheny, Lycoming, and Westmoreland county records depending on township location. Iron furnace records and early industry documents are at the historical society. Many families are Scotch-Irish Presbyterian or German Lutheran/Reformed. The Redbank Valley and Mahoning Creek areas were settled by families moving north from the Cumberland Valley. Check FamilySearch for microfilmed church and tax records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed indexes from 1800; contact office for online availability

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1800.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Armstrong County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1800. Includes manufacturing schedules for iron and glass industries.

source_profiles.source_name: Presbyterian and Lutheran Records — Armstrong Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Scotch-Irish Presbyterian and German Lutheran church records. Some Covenanter/Reformed Presbyterian.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Partial survival. FamilySearch has key collections.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kittanning newspapers with local vital notices, legal advertisements, estate notices.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny and Washington counties. Located at confluence of Beaver River and Ohio River northwest of Pittsburgh. Industrial county with steel, zinc, and ceramics manufacturing. Significant immigrant populations from Eastern Europe and Italy working in mills and factories.
county_metadata.research_tips: Beaver County Recorder of Deeds holds records from 1800. The Beaver County Genealogy and History Center in the Carnegie Free Library is the primary genealogical resource with surname files, cemetery records, and church record compilations. For pre-1800 research check Allegheny and Washington counties. The steel industry (especially in Aliquippa, Ambridge, and Midland) generated company records documenting immigrant workers. St. Nicholas Croatian Catholic Church in Millvale and other ethnic parishes have records of Slavic immigrants. Check NARA Philadelphia for naturalization records. The Harmony Society records at Old Economy Village in Ambridge document the German communal settlement (1824-1905).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1800; online index available

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation with online index.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1800.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Beaver County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1800. Manufacturing schedules document industrial growth.
Multi-ethnic church records including Croatian, Slovak, Italian Catholic parishes, Lutheran, and Presbyterian congregations. Harmony Society records at Old Economy Village.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Variable by parish. Harmony Society records well-preserved.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Beaver County Times and predecessors with obituaries, estate notices, and industrial news.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1771 from Cumberland County, one of the earliest western PA counties. Named for Fort Bedford (1758). Gateway to western frontier during colonial and early national period. Parent county for many western PA counties. Strong Scotch-Irish settlement. Bedford Springs was a famous resort visited by presidents.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bedford County is a critical parent county for western PA genealogy since Huntingdon, Somerset, Fayette, and other counties were carved from it. The Bedford County Historical Society maintains genealogical files and publishes Pioneer Record. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1771, including some of the oldest land records in western PA. For pre-1771 families, check Cumberland County records. The Scotch-Irish Presbyterian heritage dominates; check First Presbyterian Church records in Bedford. German Lutheran and Reformed families settled the valleys. The Bedford County Heritage Commission documents frontier-era sites. Forbes Road and early turnpike records document transportation through the county.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Bedford, Somerset and Fulton Counties, Pennsylvania (1884)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1771; among oldest in western PA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1771, among the oldest in western Pennsylvania.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1771. Critical for western PA land research.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1771. Pre-Cumberland County formation records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete. Early volumes valuable for frontier families.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Bedford County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1790. Tax lists supplement census for pre-1790 period.
Provincial and county tax lists. Critical for identifying settlers before first census.
Scotch-Irish Presbyterian records predominate. Some German Reformed and Lutheran.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1752 from Chester, Lancaster, and Philadelphia counties. Heart of Pennsylvania Dutch country. Reading was a major industrial city (railroad, iron, textiles). German immigration from 1700s made this one of the most German counties in America. Home to Schwenkfelders, Mennonites, Amish, German Reformed, and Lutheran communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Berks County research is deeply tied to German heritage. The Historical Society of Berks County in Reading has extensive genealogical collections including church records, family files, and the Berks County Genealogical Society collection. The Schwenkfelder Library and Heritage Center in Pennsburg (Montgomery Co) holds Schwenkfelder records relevant to Berks families. For German Reformed and Lutheran church records, FamilySearch has extensive microfilm collections. The Reading Public Library has local history materials. Recorder of Deeds records from 1752 are available. For pre-1752 families, check Chester, Lancaster, or Philadelphia county records. The Pennsylvania German Society publications are essential reference works. Dialect writers and folk art traditions document unique cultural heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1752; online index system available

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1752 with online index. Among oldest county records in PA.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories. Many early wills in German language.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1752. German-language wills require translation skills.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Berks County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.

source_profiles.source_name: German Reformed and Lutheran Church Records — Berks Co
source_profiles.repository: Historical Society of Berks County / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Extensive German Reformed, Lutheran, Moravian, and Schwenkfelder baptism, marriage, burial records. Some of the most complete German church records in America.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival. Many published in PA German Society volumes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1790. German-language names may vary in spelling across enumerations.
Provincial and county tax assessment lists from 1752.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival. Essential for pre-1790 research.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Reading Eagle, Reading Times, German-language newspapers including Readinger Adler.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good coverage. German-language papers valuable for PA Dutch research.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1846 from Huntingdon and Bedford counties. Named for John Blair, prominent citizen. Contains Altoona, a major Pennsylvania Railroad hub. The Horseshoe Curve (1854) was an engineering marvel. Railroad, iron, and coal industries defined the economy. Significant Irish and Eastern European immigration for railroad and industrial work.
county_metadata.research_tips: Blair County research centers on the Blair County Historical Society in Altoona, which has railroad-related genealogical materials including Pennsylvania Railroad employment records. Recorder of Deeds records from 1846 are in Hollidaysburg. For pre-1846 families check Huntingdon and Bedford counties. Railroad workers from Ireland, Italy, and Eastern Europe are documented in company records and ethnic parish records. The Altoona Area Public Library has local history collections. Baker Mansion in Altoona houses the historical society. Check Catholic parish records for Irish and Italian immigrants. The Pennsylvania Railroad heritage makes Blair unique for industrial genealogy.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1846.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Blair County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1850. Railroad workers and iron workers documented in manufacturing schedules.

source_profiles.source_name: Catholic and Protestant Records — Blair County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Irish Catholic, Italian Catholic, Lutheran, Presbyterian, Methodist records. Railroad worker communities.
Altoona Tribune, Altoona Mirror with railroad news, obituaries, and community notices.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1810 from Luzerne and Lycoming counties. Originally named Ontario County, renamed Bradford in 1812. Northern Tier county on New York border. Connecticut settlers from Susquehanna Company claims. Timber industry major economic driver. Coal mining in parts of county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bradford County research should begin at the Bradford County Historical Society in Towanda, which maintains family files and local records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1810. For pre-1810 families check Luzerne and Lycoming counties. Connecticut claimants under the Susquehanna Company are documented in the Susquehanna Company Papers (published). New England heritage means Congregational and Baptist church records are important alongside Methodist and Presbyterian. Timber industry records document lumbermen and their families. The Northern Tier connection to New York means checking Tioga and Chemung County NY records for cross-border families. Towanda's newspapers are on Chronicling America.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1810.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Bradford County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1810. Timber industry workers documented.

source_profiles.source_name: Baptist and Methodist Records — Bradford Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: New England-heritage Baptist, Congregational, and Methodist church records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Partial survival. FamilySearch has some microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Towanda Daily Review and Bradford County newspapers with legal notices and vital records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the three original counties created by William Penn in 1682. Named for Buckinghamshire, England. Quaker settlement from earliest period. The Welsh Tract extended into parts of Bucks County. Washington's famous crossing of the Delaware occurred here in 1776. Significant artistic community in New Hope area from 1900s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bucks County is among the most genealogically rich counties in America due to its Quaker heritage. The Bucks County Historical Society (Mercer Museum) in Doylestown is the premier research facility with the Spruance Library containing deeds, wills, church records, and family files dating to the 1680s. Friends Historical Library at Swarthmore College holds Bucks County Quaker meeting records including births, deaths, marriages, disownments, and certificates of removal from the 1680s. Recorder of Deeds has colonial records from 1684. For Welsh Tract families, check the Welsh heritage collections. The Moravian Archives in Bethlehem hold records of Moravian families in upper Bucks. Doylestown's Fonthill Castle archives contain Henry Mercer's research on early PA settlers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Colonial deed records from 1684; one of oldest in PA. Online search available

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial-era deed records from 1684. Penn's original land grants. Online index.
Wills, administrations, inventories from 1684. Some of the oldest probate records in PA.
Bucks Monthly Meeting, Middletown, Falls, Wrightstown, Buckingham meetings. Births, marriages, deaths, disownments, removals from 1681.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptional preservation. Quaker records are among best in America.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Bucks County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1790. Colonial tax lists supplement pre-census records.

source_profiles.repository: PA State Archives / PA Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original warrants, surveys, and patents under Penn proprietary system. Critical for colonial land ownership.
Doylestown Intelligencer, Bucks County Gazette with vital records, estate notices, legal advertisements.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny County. Named for General Richard Butler. Located north of Pittsburgh. Significant oil and gas production in 19th century. Harmony Society (German communal group) first settlement at Harmony (1804-1814) before moving to Economy. Agricultural county with some industrial development.
county_metadata.research_tips: Butler County Historical Society maintains genealogical files, cemetery transcriptions, and local history collections. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1800. For pre-1800 research check Allegheny County. The Harmony Museum preserves records of the Harmony Society's original settlement (1804-1814) including membership lists and financial records. Presbyterian and Lutheran church records are important sources. The oil industry (1860s+) brought workers documented in industry records. Butler County Community College has local history materials. Connoquenessing Creek valley families are documented in early tax and deed records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1800.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Butler County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1800.

source_profiles.source_name: Presbyterian and Lutheran Records — Butler Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Presbyterian (Scotch-Irish settlers) and Lutheran (German settlers) church records.
Butler newspapers with legal notices, obituaries, and community news.
